page.title: Marbella Wine Tasting Yacht Cruise: Sip and Sail

data.meta_description: Book a Marbella wine tasting yacht cruise from Puerto Banús. Sip fine Spanish wines on a private boat with skipper, snacks & drinks included from €749.
data.summary: Combine luxury sailing with Spanish wine culture on a private yacht cruise from Puerto Banús. Each charter includes a licensed skipper, curated wine selection, tapas-style snacks, and soft drinks – prices start at €749 for 2 hours.
key_facts: Private charter with licensed skipper included
Spanish wines, cava, beer, soft drinks & water provided
Up to 11 guests on select boats (e.g. Azimut 39)
Fuel, insurance & IVA (21%) all included – no hidden fees

faq.q: What wines are served on the Marbella wine tasting yacht cruise?
faq.a: We provide a selection of Spanish wines including a crisp Albariño (Rías Baixas), a bold Rioja Crianza, and a chilled cava from Penedès. The exact bottles vary by season, but always feature DO-certified wines. You can also request a specific preference (e.g. all white) when booking – just let us know.

faq.q: Can we bring our own wine on board?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring your own wine. There is no corkage fee. However, we already include unlimited water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava – so most guests find the included selection sufficient. If you want a specific vintage or rare bottle, feel free to bring it.

faq.q: How long does the wine tasting yacht cruise last?
faq.a: Our standard wine tasting cruises are available from 2 hours up to a full day (8 hours). The 2-hour option (€749) is perfect for a sunset tasting near the Marbella coastline. For a more relaxed experience with swimming stops, we recommend 4 hours or more.

faq.q: What boats are available for the wine tasting experience?
faq.a: The Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 guests) and Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 guests) are our most popular choices for wine tasting cruises, starting at €749/2h. For larger groups (up to 12 guests), consider the Mangusta 80 (24m, from €4,719/4h) which includes a jet ski. All boats depart from Puerto Banús.

faq.q: Is the wine tasting guided or self-serve?
faq.a: It is self-serve – your skipper will hand you the bottles and glasses, and you are free to pour as you wish. No sommelier is included, but the skipper can share basic info about the wines if asked. For a guided tasting with a professional, we recommend booking a dedicated event package (contact us via WhatsApp).

faq.q: Can we swim or stop for a swim during the cruise?
faq.a: Yes – swimming stops are possible depending on sea conditions and time. Popular spots include Cala del Faro (west of Marbella) or Cala Cortés near Cabopino. Your skipper will choose a calm, safe cove. Towels are not provided, so bring your own.

We offer free cancellation up to 48 hours before departure. Within 48 hours, a 50% charge applies. In case of bad weather (e.g. storms, high winds), we will reschedule or refund in full. We recommend travel insurance for last-minute changes.

faq.q: How do I book the Marbella wine tasting yacht cruise?
faq.a: Contact us via WhatsApp or the booking form on our website. Provide your preferred date, group size, and boat choice. We will confirm availability and send a payment link. Prices are all-inclusive (skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, IVA).

data.body_html:
Marbella is one of Europe's finest destinations for a wine tasting yacht cruise. Departing from the glamorous Puerto Banús, you can sip chilled Albariño and Rioja while cruising the Costa del Sol's golden coastline. Our private charters include a licensed skipper, a curated selection of Spanish wines, cava, beer, and tapas-style snacks – all from €749 for 2 hours (up to 11 guests). No hidden fees, no crowds, just you, your friends, and the Mediterranean.

Why Choose a Wine Tasting Yacht Cruise in Marbella?

A wine tasting cruise combines two of Marbella's greatest pleasures: luxury sailing and Spanish wine culture. Instead of a static tasting room, you get a moving terrace with panoramic views of La Concha mountain, the Golden Mile, and the Marbella coastline. The gentle sea breeze enhances the aromas of your wine, and you can stop for a swim at a secluded cove like Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés. Our all-inclusive pricing means you don't worry about extras – everything from the skipper to the fuel to the VAT is covered.

What’s Included in Your Wine Tasting Cruise

  • Licensed skipper – your captain handles navigation and safety
  • Spanish wine selection – includes Albariño, Rioja Crianza, and cava (or your preference)
  • Unlimited soft drinks, water, beer & cava
  • Light snacks – olives, almonds, manchego cheese, and breadsticks
  • Fuel and insurance – no surprise charges
  • IVA (21% Spanish VAT) – already included in the price
  • Free cancellation up to 48 hours

Boats for Your Wine Tasting Experience

We offer a range of private yachts from our fleet, all departing from Puerto Banús. For a romantic sunset tasting, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) are perfect at €749/2h. For larger groups, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) includes a jet ski and starts at €4,719 for 4 hours. Other boats like the Pershing 46 (14m, 10 pax) or Fairline Targa 12m (12m, 10 pax) are available on request. See our full fleet for details.

Popular Itineraries for Your Wine Tasting Cruise

Your skipper can tailor the route to your preferences. Popular options include:

  • Sunset cruise along the Golden Mile – 2 hours, past Marbella Marina and the iconic Puente Romano
  • West to Estepona – 4 hours, with a swimming stop at Cala del Faro or Playa del Cristo
  • East to Cabopino – 4 hours, explore the sand dunes and Cala Cortés
  • Full-day to Sotogrande – 8 hours, lunch at a beach club, wine tasting on board

All itineraries include time to relax, swim, and enjoy your wine. Just let your skipper know your preferences.

How to Book Your Marbella Wine Tasting Yacht Cruise

Booking is simple. Contact us via WhatsApp or the form on our homepage with your preferred date, group size, and boat choice. We will confirm availability and send a secure payment link. Prices are all-inclusive – no hidden fees. For groups larger than 12, we can arrange multiple boats or a larger yacht like the Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) or Canados 86 (26m, 12 pax).

Frequently Asked Questions

What wines are served on the Marbella wine tasting yacht cruise?

We provide a selection of Spanish wines including a crisp Albariño (Rías Baixas), a bold Rioja Crianza, and a chilled cava from Penedès. The exact bottles vary by season, but always feature DO-certified wines. You can also request a specific preference (e.g. all white) when booking – just let us know.

Can we bring our own wine on board?

Yes, you are welcome to bring your own wine. There is no corkage fee. However, we already include unlimited water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava – so most guests find the included selection sufficient. If you want a specific vintage or rare bottle, feel free to bring it.

How long does the wine tasting yacht cruise last?

Our standard wine tasting cruises are available from 2 hours up to a full day (8 hours). The 2-hour option (€749) is perfect for a sunset tasting near the Marbella coastline. For a more relaxed experience with swimming stops, we recommend 4 hours or more.

What boats are available for the wine tasting experience?

The Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 guests) and Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 guests) are our most popular choices for wine tasting cruises, starting at €749/2h. For larger groups (up to 12 guests), consider the Mangusta 80 (24m, from €4,719/4h) which includes a jet ski. All boats depart from Puerto Banús.

Is the wine tasting guided or self-serve?

It is self-serve – your skipper will hand you the bottles and glasses, and you are free to pour as you wish. No sommelier is included, but the skipper can share basic info about the wines if asked. For a guided tasting with a professional, we recommend booking a dedicated event package (contact us via WhatsApp).

Can we swim or stop for a swim during the cruise?

Yes – swimming stops are possible depending on sea conditions and time. Popular spots include Cala del Faro (west of Marbella) or Cala Cortés near Cabopino. Your skipper will choose a calm, safe cove. Towels are not provided, so bring your own.

What is the cancellation policy?

We offer free cancellation up to 48 hours before departure. Within 48 hours, a 50% charge applies. In case of bad weather (e.g. storms, high winds), we will reschedule or refund in full. We recommend travel insurance for last-minute changes.

How do I book the Marbella wine tasting yacht cruise?

Contact us via WhatsApp or the booking form on our website. Provide your preferred date, group size, and boat choice. We will confirm availability and send a payment link. Prices are all-inclusive (skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, IVA).
